task_statement:
Full redo of Global Operating Rule 8 (fan-in/fan-out barrier admission checker), starting from current main which has NONE of the prior 0701a attempt landed (0701a was declared, built, and correctly NOT adopted after independent verification found a pin_estate_integrity rejection; 0701b then incorrectly assumed 0701a had landed and tried to patch a file state that does not exist -- COO process error, not a Building defect). This Building does the full original scope in one clean pass, with the specific mistake from 0701a called out so it is not repeated.

Implement a new checker (support/checkers/lib/ + registration in check_profile.py + module_registry.yaml row, matching existing checker conventions) that statically inspects a graph_packet plan (nodes/edges/groups, matching support/operator/plan_graph.py shapes) and rejects at admission time any fan_in group whose fan_in_target_ref is the direct source of an immediately-following fan_out group with zero intervening nodes (fan-in and fan-out must never be the same event, per agent/skills/building-coordination/SKILL.md).

MISTAKE TO AVOID (this sank 0701a): do NOT add a profile-YAML rule (path_exists or otherwise) that merely references the fixture files (project/brick-protocol/status/kernel/GOAL/brick-6-p7-easy-building-ergonomics-0701d.json /-0701f.json) for documentation/traceability purposes. support/checkers/lib/pin_estate_integrity rejects any profile rule whose only assertion is path-existence on a history doc with no accompanying content pin, as decoration. The fixture files are read directly by the new checker module's own Python source (or its test/probe code) -- they do not need a separate profile-YAML existence pin. If you want the checker's own negative/positive fixtures documented anywhere, put them as inline Python constants or a docstring in the checker module itself, or as kernel-check-level logic, never as a bare profile-YAML path_exists rule.

Negative fixture (must RED-fire): reconstruct the P7d (0701d) shape where docs-lane-qa and checker-lane-qa fan directly into three final-QA nodes with no barrier. Positive fixture (must stay accepted): the P7d2/P7d3 (0701e/0701f) shape with the lane-qa-fanin-confirm barrier.

Verification requirement (this is what actually failed last time -- be thorough): run py_compile, the new focused profile, AND check_profile.py --all to completion. If this sandbox cannot get a writable temp directory (a known QA/probe_write capability constraint in this checkout, tracked separately in brick-6-graph-route-missing-default-write-scope-finding-0701.md), say so explicitly rather than claiming completion -- COO will independently re-verify in a clean detached worktree regardless before adopting.
selection_rule: Low-risk, purely additive checker task (goal doc tiering: low-risk/doc/cleanup phases may use a simpler shape than the mandatory dual-design fan-out reserved for named high-risk phases). Single design, single work, three-way final QA fan-out, closure.

nodes.work_statement: Design the fan-in/fan-out-barrier admission checker. Read agent/skills/building-coordination/SKILL.md, support/operator/plan_graph.py, and the two fixture graph JSON files. Explicitly plan to NOT add any profile-YAML pin referencing the fixture files -- design where the fixture reference lives (inside the checker module itself). No source edits.

Implement the checker per the accepted design. Checker-first: add the negative fixture first, prove it currently passes/is not caught (the bug exists), implement the rejection, prove the negative fixture now RED-fires as expected and the positive fixture stays accepted. Register in check_profile.py. Run py_compile, the new focused profile, and check_profile.py --all. Return changed_files and exact command output.

Attack the checker implementation: confirm the negative fixture genuinely RED-fires with the new checker (not vacuously), confirm the positive fixture (P7d2/P7d3 barrier shape) is still accepted, confirm py_compile and check_profile.py --all pass. Probe_write only, no source mutation.

Confirm this checker stays support/checker mechanics only -- it may REJECT a bad graph_packet, but must not choose Movement, targets, success, or quality; it must not become a second engine or a second admission path duplicating plan_graph.py. Probe_write only.

Confirm evidence integrity: negative/positive fixtures are real and byte-distinct from prior QA bodies, proof_limits/not_proven present, no overclaiming of coverage beyond the fan-in/fan-out-barrier shape checked. Probe_write only.

Fan in code-qa, axis-qa, evidence-qa. Report observed_evidence / narrowly_proven / not_proven / next Movement candidate. Close only if the negative fixture RED-fires and the positive fixture stays accepted with current evidence; otherwise return Link-facing transition_concern_evidence and HOLD rather than smoothing it.

proof_limits: official brick build graph evidence is support evidence only
this Building closes only Global Operating Rule 8 (fan-in/fan-out barrier admission), not whole P8/P9 readiness
not source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, or Movement authority
not_proven: whether this checker catches every possible malformed graph topology, only the specific fan-in-immediately-fan-out shape
P8 ship safety, P9 dynamic customer replay, fresh-machine install, real-provider reliability, customer comprehension
